html:
Exclusive to:
Only during the Event: Year of the Beefalo

The Sewing Machine Kit is a craftable Item from the Offerings Filter, made with a Stinger, one Gold Nugget, and two Silk. It can be deployed into a Sewing Machine, which is used to create Costume Patterns from Pattern Scraps. To use it, three Pattern Scraps need to be placed inside.

Pattern Scrap Combinations
Resulting Costume
Formal Formal Formal Formal Costume Pattern
Festive Festive Festive Festive Costume Pattern
Fearsome Fearsome Fearsome Ironclad Costume Pattern
Formal Formal Festive Dolled-Up Costume Pattern
Formal Formal Fearsome No Result
Festive Festive Formal Flowery Costume Pattern
Festive Festive Fearsome Lucky Beast Costume Pattern
Fearsome Fearsome Formal Warrior Costume Pattern
Fearsome Fearsome Festive Frostbitten Costume Pattern
Formal Festive Fearsome Victorian Costume Pattern

Contents

  • 1 Pattern Scraps
  • 2 Costume Patterns
    • 2.1 Warrior Costume Pattern
    • 2.2 Dolled-Up Costume Pattern
    • 2.3 Festive Costume Pattern
    • 2.4 Ironclad Costume Pattern
    • 2.5 Flowery Costume Pattern
    • 2.6 Formal Costume Pattern
    • 2.7 Victorian Costume Pattern
    • 2.8 Lucky Beast Costume Pattern
    • 2.9 Frostbitten Costume Pattern
  • 3 Beefalo Dolls
  • 4 Gallery

Pattern Scraps

Pattern Scraps can be acquired in exchange for 5 Lucky Gold Nuggets in the Offerings Filter. Also, random scraps are given out by the Judge after a Beefalo Pageant. There are three types: Fearsome, Formal And Festive. They are to be put into a Sewing Machine in various combinations to produce Costume Patterns. If the scrap combination is invalid, the machine will return all the scraps placed inside. The following table shows the possible valid combinations.

Costume Patterns

Costume Patterns operate similarly to Blueprints. The player can right click on the Pattern to learn a new set of Beefalo Skins, as well as produce one Beefalo Doll wearing the matching skin set. New Patterns learned will make a pop-up appear in the upper-left corner of the screen, in a similar manner to the pop-up that notifies players of an in-game skin drop. The player can still right-click patterns even after learning them to continue acquiring Beefalo Dolls.

The Beefalo Skins can be accessed at the Grooming Station. The skin selection screen is the same as the Wardrobe. For instance, Skin presets can be saved.

Beefalo Skins differ from other skins in that they are not found in the Curio Cabinet. They are only accessible in-game, and are not remembered across games; they have to be re-learned in each game like other tiered crafts. All Beefalo Skins are of the exclusive Complimentary rarity. They cannot be bought with real monies, woven, traded, or sold on the Marketplace.

Warrior Costume Pattern

The Warrior Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Fearsome Pattern Scraps and one Formal Pattern Scrap.

  • Complimentary
    Warrior Caparison
    Strike fear into the hearts of your enemies!
  • Complimentary
    Warrior Horns
    The only downside to these mighty war horns is you can't play them.
  • Complimentary
    Warrior Headgear
    Reserved for only the most bull-headed warriors.
  • Complimentary
    Warrior Shoes
    A steed must have sturdy footwear before going into battle.
  • Complimentary
    Warrior Tail
    This tail has been tied back to keep it out of the way in battle.

Dolled-Up Costume Pattern

The Dolled-Up Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Formal Pattern Scraps and one Festive Pattern Scrap.

  • Complimentary
    Dolled-Up Caparison
    Isn't it just darling?
    See ingame
  • Complimentary
    Dolled-Up Horns
    These horns are curli-cute!
    See ingame
  • Complimentary
    Dolled-Up Headgear
    Style your beefalo with adorable ringlets and ribbons.
    See ingame
  • Complimentary
    Dolled-Up Shoes
    The daintiest beefalo shoes you ever did see!
    See ingame
  • Complimentary
    Dolled-Up Tail
    This tail is tied with a darling pink ribbon.
    See ingame

Festive Costume Pattern

The Festive Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with three Festive Pattern Scraps.

  • Complimentary
    Festive Caparison
    All pom-pom and circumstance!
  • Complimentary
    Festive Horns
    These horns are adorned with colorful ribbons.
  • Complimentary
    Festive Headgear
    Give your beefalo a pair of pom-pom pigtails.
  • Complimentary
    Festive Shoes
    Footloose and fancy feet!
  • Complimentary
    Festive Tail
    A poofy pom-pom tail.

Ironclad Costume Pattern

The Ironclad Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with three Fearsome Pattern Scraps.

  • Complimentary
    Ironclad Caparison
    It gives new meaning to the phrase "iron stomach".
  • Complimentary
    Ironclad Horns
    Industrial strength horns for the industrious beefalo.
  • Complimentary
    Ironclad Headgear
    Beefalo are hard-headed creatures even at the best of times.
  • Complimentary
    Ironclad Shoes
    Put some spring in your beefalo's step!
  • Complimentary
    Ironclad Tail
    An exceptionally wiry beefalo tail.

Flowery Costume Pattern

The Flowery Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Festive Pattern Scraps and one Formal Pattern Scrap.

  • Complimentary
    Flowery Caparison
    Your beefalo might be tempted to take a bite out of it.
  • Complimentary
    Flowery Horns
    With these floral horns, your beefalo will be there with bells on!
  • Complimentary
    Flowery Headgear
    Adorn your beefalo with a sweet-smelling crown of flowers.
  • Complimentary
    Flowery Shoes
    Gives your beefalo a simple, all-natural look.
  • Complimentary
    Flowery Tail
    A braided tail, decorated with wildflowers.

Formal Costume Pattern

The Formal Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with three Formal Pattern Scraps.

  • Complimentary
    Formal Caparison
    Why shouldn't a beefalo get to be dapper too?
  • Complimentary
    Formal Horns
    Horns that evoke the feeling of a twirled mustache.
  • Complimentary
    Formal Headgear
    It's rare to see a beefalo with such perfectly coiffed fur.
  • Complimentary
    Formal Shoes
    Sport some sophisticated spats.
  • Complimentary
    Formal Tail
    Even tails are fancier with a cravat.

Victorian Costume Pattern

The Victorian Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with one Formal Pattern Scrap, one Festive Pattern Scrap, and one Fearsome Pattern Scrap.

  • Complimentary
    Victorian Caparison
    You really can't go wrong with ruffles.
  • Complimentary
    Victorian Horns
    Meticulously polished to be as gleaming white as your best bone china.
  • Complimentary
    Victorian Headgear
    No Victorian ensemble is complete without a fine hat.
  • Complimentary
    Victorian Shoes
    It might behoove you to adorn your beefalo in these ruffled cuffs.
  • Complimentary
    Victorian Tail
    It is imperative for one to brush a beefalo's tail often to maintain its softness and natural luster.

Lucky Beast Costume Pattern

The Lucky Beast Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Festive Pattern Scraps and one Fearsome Pattern Scrap.

  • Complimentary
    Lucky Beast Caparison
    Add a little luck to your beefalo's look.
  • Complimentary
    Lucky Beast Headgear
    This beefalo is lucky that the beast is just a costume.
  • Complimentary
    Lucky Beast Shoes
    These lucky shoes will take you places!
  • Complimentary
    Lucky Beast Tail
    This fanciful covering gives your beefalo's tail a more lizard-like appearance.

Frostbitten Costume Pattern

The Frostbitten Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Fearsome Pattern Scraps and one Festive Pattern Scrap.

  • Complimentary
    Frostbitten Caparison
    A woolly blanket for a chilly beefalo.
  • Complimentary
    Frostbitten Horns
    A cold winter gale has frozen these horns solid.
  • Complimentary
    Frostbitten Headgear
    That fuzzy face could make the coldest heart melt.
  • Complimentary
    Frostbitten Shoes
    Looks like this beefalo's getting cold feet.
  • Complimentary
    Frostbitten Tail
    A fluffy winter tail.

Beefalo Dolls

When a Pattern is learned, a Beefalo Doll will spawn. The type of Beefalo Doll depends on the type of Pattern learned. Beefalo Dolls can be appraised by the Judge, which will provide clues as to what style of Beefalo will win that day.

Gallery

  • Unused Beefalo Doll icon
  • Unused Beefalo Doll

"yotb_sewingmachine_item"
“Sewing can't be that hard... can it?”
–Wilson
“I never liked sewing.”
–Willow
“Will sew loveliest of costumes for hairy cow.”
–Wolfgang
“Mother liked to sew matching outfits for Abigail and I.”
–Wendy
“HAIRY MINION COSTUME FABRICATOR”
–WX-78
“I tend to prefer crochet.”
–Wickerbottom
“I'm pretty handy with a sewing needle.”
–Woodie
“I'm more of an appreciator of fine clothes than a maker of them.”
–Maxwell
“The cömpetitiön will be fierce, thus my beast's öutfits must be fiercer!”
–Wigfrid
“We're really good with silk and threads!”
–Webber
“Now to find the recipe for a tasteful outfit.”
–Warly
“Clothes maker”
–Wormwood
“I'm more handy with tape than a needle...”
–Winona
“It's all connected by a common thread, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Make fancy dress-up things!”
–Wurt
“My mom has one of those! It looks a bit different, though.”
–Walter
“At least it's faster than sewing by hand.”
–Wanda

"yotb_pattern_fragment_1-3"
“If I put some of these together, I bet I could make a beefalo costume.”
–Wilson
“Looks like I'll have to put a few of these together to make anything worthwhile.”
–Willow
“Is bit of pattern. Wolfgang should find more.”
–Wolfgang
“Merely a lonely part of a completed whole.”
–Wendy
“DATA INCOMPLETE, MUST ACQUIRE ADDITIONAL FASHION SCRAPS”
–WX-78
“Merely a single piece of a larger costuming pattern.”
–Wickerbottom
“Looks like I'll have to gather a few of these to make a whole pattern.”
–Woodie
“I'll have to combine them before they'll reveal their secrets.”
–Maxwell
“Tis but a fragment of a cömplete pattern.”
–Wigfrid
“It tells us how to make a beefalo costume. Well, part of one...”
–Webber
“One part of a recipe for a beefalo costume.”
–Warly
“Huh? Not finished”
–Wormwood
“Looks like a set of instructions, I should try putting a few together.”
–Winona
“A puzzling piece of a pattern.”
–Wortox
“Hey, where the rest?”
–Wurt
“This looks like instructions for a beefalo costume! Only part of one, though...”
–Walter
“With a few more pieces, I could fashion a new pattern.”
–Wanda

Warrior Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“How ferocious!”
–Wilson
“Ooooh, looks dangerous!”
–Willow
“Yes, will make hairy cow fearsome!”
–Wolfgang
“Even seemingly gentle creatures have a dark side.”
–Wendy
“EXCELLENT. THIS WILL DO NICELY”
–WX-78
“It's rather aggressive looking.”
–Wickerbottom
“Pretty intimidating, eh Lucy?”
–Woodie
“This could be useful.”
–Maxwell
“Garb befitting a warriör's steed!”
–Wigfrid
“Our beefalo might look scary in this, but we know they're really a big softy!”
–Webber
“Dressing for some rather tough beef!”
–Warly
“Scary!”
–Wormwood
“I don't know if I want to encourage my beefalo's ornery side.”
–Winona
“Fit for a fearsome fellow.”
–Wortox
“Hee-hee good and scary!”
–Wurt
“I wonder if the beefalo actually like wearing these.”
–Walter
“I wouldn't mind having a beefalo bodyguard.”
–Wanda

Dolled-Up Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“My beefalo will look as cute as a button!”
–Wilson
“Awww, my beefalo's gonna be so hideous and adorable!”
–Willow
“Will make hairy cow so cute!”
–Wolfgang
“It's pretty.”
–Wendy
“A DISARMING APPEARANCE COULD BE USEFUL”
–WX-78
“A bit childish for my tastes.”
–Wickerbottom
“I can doll it up all I want, it'll still be a beefalo.”
–Woodie
“I can only imagine how grotesque this will look...”
–Maxwell
“Whö wöuld wear this intö battle?”
–Wigfrid
“So many ruffles...”
–Webber
“Tres mignon!”
–Warly
“Aww”
–Wormwood
“That's a lot of ruffles to sew on...”
–Winona
“Oh what a dollight! Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Look kind of creepy...”
–Wurt
“Aww, it's cute!”
–Walter
“I don't remember the dolls from my childhood being quite so big and hairy.”
–Wanda
The Dolled-Up Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Formal Pattern Scraps and one Festive Pattern Scrap.

Festive Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“This is just the occasion for some festivity!”
–Wilson
“It needs more firecrackers before I'd call it "festive".”
–Willow
“Is made from brightest of colors!”
–Wolfgang
“It's so colorful, it hurts my eyes.”
–Wendy
“FRIVOLOUS”
–WX-78
“It features quite an eclectic combination of colors.”
–Wickerbottom
“Looks pretty cheerful!”
–Woodie
“Positively garish.”
–Maxwell
“The bright cölörs cöuld be useful för distracting enemies.”
–Wigfrid
“This is going to be so much fun!”
–Webber
“Just the right thing for a celebration.”
–Warly
“Pretty”
–Wormwood
“Sure is a cheery looking design!”
–Winona
“My friend can frolic in this festive frock!”
–Wortox
“Big fuzzy gonna look so good in this!”
–Wurt
“I wish I could make one for Woby, but her measurements keep changing...”
–Walter
“This outfit's louder than a chiming grandfather clock.”
–Wanda
The Festive Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with three Festive Pattern Scraps.
Complimentary
Festive Caparison
All pom-pom and circumstance!
Complimentary
Festive Horns
These horns are adorned with colorful ribbons.
Complimentary
Festive Headgear
Give your beefalo a pair of pom-pom pigtails.
Complimentary
Festive Shoes
Footloose and fancy feet!
Complimentary
Festive Tail
A poofy pom-pom tail.
Ironclad Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“This requires a suspicious amount of welding for a sewing project.”
–Wilson
“Uh, is this really a sewing pattern?”
–Willow
“Will make hairy cow tough like iron!”
–Wolfgang
“Oh to be encased within a hard and unfeeling exterior...”
–Wendy
“THERE WILL STILL BE ORGANIC COMPONENTS INSIDE, BUT IT'S AN IMPROVEMENT”
–WX-78
“I'm not certain just how much sewing is actually involved.”
–Wickerbottom
“Is this, er... humane?”
–Woodie
“Is this the robot's doing?”
–Maxwell
“Armör öf irön för my steed!”
–Wigfrid
“We hope it's not too heavy for the beefalo.”
–Webber
“This does not look like any sewing pattern I've ever seen.”
–Warly
“Machine?”
–Wormwood
“Now this one I can do!”
–Winona
“This outfit might require a lot of ironing, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Make big fuzzy look like Ironfolk?”
–Wurt
“Is this really for sewing?”
–Walter
“I might need a screwdriver instead of a sewing needle.”
–Wanda
The Ironclad Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with three Fearsome Pattern Scraps.
Complimentary
Ironclad Caparison
It gives new meaning to the phrase "iron stomach".
Complimentary
Ironclad Horns
Industrial strength horns for the industrious beefalo.
Complimentary
Ironclad Headgear
Beefalo are hard-headed creatures even at the best of times.
Complimentary
Ironclad Shoes
Put some spring in your beefalo's step!
Complimentary
Ironclad Tail
An exceptionally wiry beefalo tail.
Flowery Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“You really can't go wrong with flowers.”
–Wilson
“Once the flowers dry out they'll make nice kindling.”
–Willow
“Flowers would be good against cow smell.”
–Wolfgang
“Do you like this one, Abigail?”
–Wendy
“DISGUSTING”
–WX-78
“How delightful!”
–Wickerbottom
“All-natural and organic.”
–Woodie
“Florals. How saccharine.”
–Maxwell
“Flöwers wön't prötect yöu in battle!”
–Wigfrid
“Flowers are always nice.”
–Webber
“Made from natural ingredients.”
–Warly
“Covered in friends”
–Wormwood
“Good thing I'm not allergic to pollen.”
–Winona
“I suppose I'll reap what I sew, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Glurgh... this one ugly...”
–Wurt
“I hope it doesn't attract too many bees.”
–Walter
“A pretty garden-variety outfit, wouldn't you say?”
–Wanda
The Flowery Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Festive Pattern Scraps and one Formal Pattern Scrap.

Flowery Caparison
Your beefalo might be tempted to take a bite out of it.

Complimentary

Complimentary

Complimentary

Formal Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“This is a costume for some Grade A beefalo.”
–Wilson
“Ugh, it looks so stuffy and proper.”
–Willow
“Who makes fancy suit for cow?”
–Wolfgang
“It looks very dignified.”
–Wendy
“I DOUBT IT WILL MAKE THE HAIRY FLESHSACK LOOK MORE DIGNIFIED”
–WX-78
“It seems rather senseless to attempt to make a beefalo appear dignified.”
–Wickerbottom
“Canadian tuxedos look a bit different.”
–Woodie
“These garments are absolutely wasted on such base creatures.”
–Maxwell
“My fearsöme steed has nö use för such a thing!”
–Wigfrid
“Our beefalo might look even more dapper than Maxwell!”
–Webber
“The beast will look as if it's going out to a fancy dinner party.”
–Warly
“Fancy”
–Wormwood
“That's a pretty fancy thing to put on a big shaggy beast.”
–Winona
“But will it really suit my beefalo? Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Ooooh, all fancy.”
–Wurt
“This doesn't seem very practical for the outdoors.”
–Walter
“My beefalo will look like the perfect gentleman.”
–Wanda
The Formal Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with three Formal Pattern Scraps.
Complimentary
Formal Caparison
Why shouldn't a beefalo get to be dapper too?
Complimentary

Complimentary

Complimentary
Formal Tail

Victorian Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“I think my grandmother wore something similar.”
–Wilson
“Ha! This is gonna look hilarious!”
–Willow
“Looks very complicated to Wolfgang.”
–Wolfgang
“Playing dress-up with a beefalo is... quite fun...”
–Wendy
“IT IS OUTDATED”
–WX-78
“Ah, this style does take me back...”
–Wickerbottom
“Pretty fancy, eh?”
–Woodie
“Hmph. Rather outdated.”
–Maxwell
“Ödd löoking armör för a beast...”
–Wigfrid
“It's so fancy! We hope it's not too hard to make...”
–Webber
“It's hard not to think about seafood with all these scalloped edges.”
–Warly
“Lots of work...”
–Wormwood
“This looks like it involves a lotta fancy needlework...”
–Winona
“Fancy, that!”
–Wortox
“Scale-less sure dress weird, florp.”
–Wurt
“This seems unnecessarily fancy.”
–Walter
“Quite a fashionable design.”
–Wanda
The Victorian Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with one Formal Pattern Scrap, one Festive Pattern Scrap, and one Fearsome Pattern Scrap.
Complimentary
Victorian Caparison
You really can't go wrong with ruffles.
Complimentary
Victorian Horns
Meticulously polished to be as gleaming white as your best bone china.
Complimentary
Victorian Headgear
No Victorian ensemble is complete without a fine hat.
Complimentary
Victorian Shoes
It might behoove you to adorn your beefalo in these ruffled cuffs.
Complimentary
Victorian Tail
It is imperative for one to brush a beefalo's tail often to maintain its softness and natural luster.
Lucky Beast Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“I'm feeling lucky about this one!”
–Wilson
“My beefalo's already a lucky beast. He gets to be my friend!”
–Willow
“Hope beast gives Wolfgang luck in contest!”
–Wolfgang
“May it give my beefalo more luck than I've had.”
–Wendy
“INFERIOR BEINGS MUST RELY ON LUCK”
–WX-78
“I'm not one to rely on luck, but a bit won't hurt in a competition.”
–Wickerbottom
“Our beast is lucky it ended up being our friend and not our dinner, eh?”
–Woodie
“I'd say luck is in rather short supply around here.”
–Maxwell
“Nö luck is needed för öne whö's skilled in battle.”
–Wigfrid
“Another costume for our lucky beefalo!”
–Webber
“The beefalo who will wear this is lucky indeed!”
–Warly
“Lucky!”
–Wormwood
“Hope it gives me some luck in the contest.”
–Winona
“Lucky, hm? Perhaps it's made with fairy gold.”
–Wortox
“Awww, big fuzzy wanna have scales too?”
–Wurt
“I wonder what kind of animal this costume is based off of?”
–Walter
“If it's so lucky, maybe I'll keep it for myself.”
–Wanda
The Lucky Beast Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Festive Pattern Scraps and one Fearsome Pattern Scrap.
Complimentary
Lucky Beast Caparison
Add a little luck to your beefalo's look.
Complimentary
Lucky Beast Headgear
This beefalo is lucky that the beast is just a costume.
Complimentary
Lucky Beast Shoes
These lucky shoes will take you places!
Complimentary
Lucky Beast Tail
This fanciful covering gives your beefalo's tail a more lizard-like appearance.
Frostbitten Costume Pattern[edit | edit source]
“I usually like my beefalo fresh, not frozen.”
–Wilson
“Don't beefalo already have hairy winter coats?”
–Willow
“Wolfgang's hair cow will face the cold mightily!”
–Wolfgang
“It reflects the coldness I feel inside.”
–Wendy
“IT WOULD BE BENEFICIAL TO HAVE A MINION THAT CAN WITHSTAND COLD TEMPERATURES”
–WX-78
“It's always prudent to have a set of winter garments.”
–Wickerbottom
“When you live up North, you get used to the cold.”
–Woodie
“Chilling.”
–Maxwell
“My beast will nöt fear the cöld.”
–Wigfrid
“Brrr, maybe we should wear gloves for this.”
–Webber
“Frozen beef? I suppose I'll have to make do.”
–Warly
“Brrr!”
–Wormwood
“Winter gear, huh? Seems practical.”
–Winona
“I just got chills! Hyuyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Don't want big fuzzy to get cold.”
–Wurt
“I guess a beefalo's own fuzzy coat isn't enough to keep warm.”
–Walter
“The poor beefalo's going to catch a chill wearing that!”
–Wanda
The Frostbitten Costume Pattern is made at the Sewing Machine with two Fearsome Pattern Scraps and one Festive Pattern Scrap.
Complimentary
Frostbitten Caparison
A woolly blanket for a chilly beefalo.
Complimentary
Frostbitten Horns
A cold winter gale has frozen these horns solid.
Complimentary
Frostbitten Headgear
That fuzzy face could make the coldest heart melt.
Complimentary
Frostbitten Shoes
Looks like this beefalo's getting cold feet.
Complimentary
Frostbitten Tail
A fluffy winter tail.

–Wilson

–Wendy

–Wickerbottom

–Woodie

–Maxwell

“We should show it to the judge!”

“Perhaps I should show it to the judge, to get a measure of his taste.”
–Warly
“Show to shaggy hidey man”
–Wormwood

–Winona
“Our judge just may have something to say.”
–Wortox
“Should show to weird scale-less hiding in tent!”
–Wurt
“What do you think, Woby? Should I show it to the judge?”
–Walter
“Where's that peculiar judge? He might have an appreciation for this.”
–Wanda
When a Pattern is learned, a Beefalo Doll will spawn. The type of Beefalo Doll depends on the type of Pattern learned. Beefalo Dolls can be appraised by the Judge, which will provide clues as to what style of Beefalo will win that day.

title: Sewing Machine
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Event|Year of the Beefalo}} {{Object Infobox |name = Sewing Machine |image = Sewing Machine.png |perk = Create Costume Patterns |spawnCode = "yotb_sewingmachine" }}{{Object Infobox |name=Sewing Machine Kit |image=Sewing Machine Kit Dropped.png |icon=Sewing Machine Kit.png |description=Prototype the perfect beefalo outfit. |ingredient1 = Stinger |multiplier1 = 1 |ingredient2 = Gold Nugget |multiplier2 = 1 |ingredient3 = Silk |multiplier3 = 2 |filter = {{Pic32|Offerings Filter}} |tier = 27 |perk = Can be placed as a [[Structure]]. |stack = Does not stack |spawnCode="yotb_sewingmachine_item"}} {{Quotes |wilson = Sewing can't be that hard... can it? |willow = I never liked sewing. |wolfgang = Will sew loveliest of costumes for hairy cow. |wendy = Mother liked to sew matching outfits for Abigail and I. |wx78 = HAIRY MINION COSTUME FABRICATOR |wickerbottom = I tend to prefer crochet. |woodie = I'm pretty handy with a sewing needle. |maxwell = I'm more of an appreciator of fine clothes than a maker of them. |wigfrid = The cömpetitiön will be fierce, thus my beast's öutfits must be fiercer! |webber = We're really good with silk and threads! |winona = I'm more handy with tape than a needle... |warly = Now to find the recipe for a tasteful outfit. |wortox = It's all connected by a common thread, hyuyu! |wormwood = Clothes maker |wurt = Make fancy dress-up things! |walter = My mom has one of those! It looks a bit different, though. |wanda = At least it's faster than sewing by hand. |choose = wx78}} The '''Sewing Machine Kit''' is a craftable [[Item]] from the [[Offerings Filter]], made with a [[Stinger]], one [[Gold Nugget]], and two [[Silk]]. It can be deployed into a Sewing Machine, which is used to create Costume Patterns from Pattern Scraps. To use it, three Pattern Scraps need to be placed inside. {| class="wikitable" |+Pattern Scrap Combinations ! ! ! !Resulting Costume |- |Formal |Formal |Formal |Formal Costume Pattern |- |Festive |Festive |Festive |Festive Costume Pattern |- |Fearsome |Fearsome |Fearsome |Ironclad Costume Pattern |- |Formal |Formal |Festive |Dolled-Up Costume Pattern |- |Formal |Formal |Fearsome |No Result |- |Festive |Festive |Formal |Flowery Costume Pattern |- |Festive |Festive |Fearsome |Lucky Beast Costume Pattern |- |Fearsome |Fearsome |Formal |Warrior Costume Pattern |- |Fearsome |Fearsome |Festive |Frostbitten Costume Pattern |- |Formal |Festive |Fearsome |Victorian Costume Pattern |} __TOC__ {{Clear}} ==Pattern Scraps== {{Object Infobox |name = Pattern Scraps |image= Fearsome Pattern Scrap Dropped.png|Fearsome Formal Pattern Scrap Dropped.png|Formal Festive Pattern Scrap Dropped.png|Festive |icon = Fearsome Pattern Scrap.png|Fearsome Formal Pattern Scrap.png|Formal Festive Pattern Scrap.png|Festive |description_tabber= Fearsome= "Get some fearsome inspiration." |-| Formal= "Get Some formal inspiration." |-| Festive= "Get some festive inspiration." |ingredient1 = Lucky Gold Nugget |multiplier1 = 5 |filter = {{Pic32|Offerings Filter}} |tier = 27 |stack = 40 |perk = Used to make Costume Patterns |droppedBy = {{Pic32|Beefalobloke|Trade Inn}}[[Trade Inn|Curio Collector]] (Judge's Booth) |spawnCode="yotb_pattern_fragment_1-3"}} {{Quotes |wilson = If I put some of these together, I bet I could make a beefalo costume. |willow = Looks like I'll have to put a few of these together to make anything worthwhile. |wolfgang = Is bit of pattern. Wolfgang should find more. |wendy = Merely a lonely part of a completed whole. |wx78 = DATA INCOMPLETE, MUST ACQUIRE ADDITIONAL FASHION SCRAPS |wickerbottom = Merely a single piece of a larger costuming pattern. |woodie = Looks like I'll have to gather a few of these to make a whole pattern. |maxwell = I'll have to combine them before they'll reveal their secrets. |wigfrid = Tis but a fragment of a cömplete pattern. |webber = It tells us how to make a beefalo costume. Well, part of one... |winona = Looks like a set of instructions, I should try putting a few together. |warly = One part of a recipe for a beefalo costume. |wortox = A puzzling piece of a pattern. |wormwood = Huh? Not finished |wurt = Hey, where the rest? |walter = This looks like instructions for a beefalo costume! Only part of one, though... |wanda = With a few more pieces, I could fashion a new pattern. |choose = walter}} '''Pattern Scraps''' can be acquired in exchange for 5 [[Lucky Gold Nugget]]s in the Offerings Filter. Also, random scraps are given out by the Judge after a [[Beefalo Pageant]]. There are three types: Fearsome, Formal And Festive. They are to be put into a Sewing Machine in various combinations to produce Costume Patterns. If the scrap combination is invalid, the machine will return all the scraps placed inside. The following table shows the possible valid combinations. {{Clear}} ==Costume Patterns== {{Object Infobox |name = Costume Patterns |icon = Costume Pattern.png |image = Costume Pattern Dropped.png |stack = Does not stack |perk = Provides Beefalo skins |spawnCode="war_blueprint"
"doll_blueprint"
"festive_blueprint"
"robot_blueprint"
"nature_blueprint"
"formal_blueprint"
"victorian_blueprint"
"beast_blueprint"
"ice_blueprint"}} '''Costume Patterns''' operate similarly to [[Blueprint]]s. The player can right click on the Pattern to learn a new set of Beefalo Skins, as well as produce one Beefalo Doll wearing the matching skin set. New Patterns learned will make a pop-up appear in the upper-left corner of the screen, in a similar manner to the pop-up that notifies players of an in-game skin drop. The player can still right-click patterns even after learning them to continue acquiring Beefalo Dolls. The Beefalo Skins can be accessed at the [[Grooming Station]]. The skin selection screen is the same as the Wardrobe. For instance, Skin presets can be saved. Beefalo Skins differ from other skins in that they are not found in the [[Curio Cabinet]]. They are only accessible in-game, and are not remembered across games; they have to be re-learned in each game like other tiered crafts. All Beefalo Skins are of the exclusive Complimentary rarity. They cannot be bought with real monies, woven, traded, or sold on the Marketplace. ===Warrior Costume Pattern=== {{Quotes |wilson = How ferocious! |willow = Ooooh, looks dangerous! |wolfgang = Yes, will make hairy cow fearsome! |wendy = Even seemingly gentle creatures have a dark side. |wx78 = EXCELLENT. THIS WILL DO NICELY |wickerbottom = It's rather aggressive looking. |woodie = Pretty intimidating, eh Lucy? |maxwell = This could be useful. |wigfrid = Garb befitting a warriör's steed! |webber = Our beefalo might look scary in this, but we know they're really a big softy! |winona = I don't know if I want to encourage my beefalo's ornery side. |warly = Dressing for some rather tough beef! |wortox = Fit for a fearsome fellow. |wormwood = Scary! |wurt = Hee-hee good and scary! |walter = I wonder if the beefalo actually like wearing these. |wanda = I wouldn't mind having a beefalo bodyguard. |choose = wigfrid}} The '''Warrior Costume Pattern''' is made at the Sewing Machine with two Fearsome Pattern Scraps and one Formal Pattern Scrap. Hyuyu! |wormwood = Aww |wurt = Look kind of creepy... |walter = Aww, it's cute! |wanda = I don't remember the dolls from my childhood being quite so big and hairy. |choose = willow}} The '''Dolled-Up Costume Pattern''' is made at the Sewing Machine with two Formal Pattern Scraps and one Festive Pattern Scrap. How saccharine. |wigfrid = Flöwers wön't prötect yöu in battle! |webber = Flowers are always nice. |winona = Good thing I'm not allergic to pollen. |warly = Made from natural ingredients. |wortox = I suppose I'll reap what I sew, hyuyu! |wormwood = Covered in friends |wurt = Glurgh... this one ugly... |walter = I hope it doesn't attract too many bees. |wanda = A pretty garden-variety outfit, wouldn't you say? |choose = wormwood}} The '''Flowery Costume Pattern''' is made at the Sewing Machine with two Festive Pattern Scraps and one Formal Pattern Scrap. Should I show it to the judge? |wanda = Where's that peculiar judge? He might have an appreciation for this. |choose = wilson}} When a Pattern is learned, a '''Beefalo Doll''' will spawn. The type of Beefalo Doll depends on the type of Pattern learned. Beefalo Dolls can be appraised by the Judge, which will provide clues as to what style of Beefalo will win that day. {{Clear}} == {{pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Beefalo Doll.png|Unused Beefalo Doll icon Beefalo Doll Dropped.png|Unused Beefalo Doll {{Lunar New Year Events}} [[Category:Lunar New Year]] [[Category:Offerings Filter]]
